text: If we do nothing, we pay the same four hours forever.

Just a quick nudge on the API document.
I am waiting for the endpoint list, and my team relies on it.
Could you get it to me by Thursday?
If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to help.
Camila, just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because we cannot start the tests.
I know, I know. It is on my list. This week, I promise.
You said that last week too. I am not angry, but my team relies on that page, and we lose a day for every day we wait.
Everyone wants something from me. I have my own deadlines.
Then let me make it small. I do not need the full document. Only the endpoint list, one page. That would unblock us.
One page I can do. Probably.
Could you get it to me by Thursday? And if it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone from my team to write it with you.
You would do that? Fine. Thursday.
Thursday. I will confirm it in writing, so nobody drops the ball, including me.
My team relies on that page to start the tests.
I do not need the full document, only the endpoint list.

Diogo, I saw your message about Camila. Before you come to me, answer one question: have you actually asked her, clearly, with a date? A message that says "any news?" is not a request. It is noise, and people ignore noise. Ask her for one specific thing, tell her what it blocks, and give her a date. Offer to help -- sometimes people go silent because the task is bigger than they said. And do not be passive-aggressive. Do not copy me on the email to make a point. If you nudge her twice, clearly, and she still does not move, then come to me and we escalate together. But not before.

Hi Camila, just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because the test phase relies on it: without that one page, my team cannot start, and we lose a day for every day we wait. I am not asking for the full document today. I need only the endpoint list, and that would unblock us immediately. Could you get it to me by Thursday? If it is bigger than we thought, tell me and I will send someone from my team to write it with you. I would rather help you than wait for you. And if Thursday does not work, give me a date that does -- I just need to know what to tell the client on Friday.

Diogo sends a soft nudge about the API document. Camila promises to do it this week, like last week. Diogo says what it blocks: his team cannot start the tests. He makes the ask smaller: only the endpoint list, one page. Finally, he gives a date and offers to send someone to help, and Camila accepts Thursday.
